ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for white chocolate as a food item; SNOMED CT coverage for confectionery is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of white chocolate is one small piece or square weighing approximately 15 g, equivalent to about one to two standard chocolate squares.

nutrient_source.title: White Chocolate — HPB Singapore Foodo Database (G00010629)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo record G00010629 for White Chocolate. The HPB record reports anomalously low energy (42 kcal/100g) and fat (0.25g/100g) compared with standard white chocolate (~539 kcal, ~30g fat/100g per USDA SR Legacy). This likely reflects a diluted preparation, a white-chocolate-flavoured beverage, or a data entry issue. Values are preserved as reported by HPB; saturated fat is estimated from USDA SR Legacy (USDA FoodData Central NDB 19904) as the HPB record does not report saturated fat breakdown. The anomaly is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 44 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white chocolate (closest analogous entry; GI range 35–44 reported for white chocolate in Atkinson et al. 2008). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (44 × 10.36 × 0.15) / 100 = 0.68, rounded to 0.65 for 15g serving (carbohydrate per serving = 10.36 × 0.15 = 1.554g; GL = 44 × 1.554 / 100 = 0.68). Note: the HPB record carbohydrate value (10.36g/100g) is anomalously low for standard white chocolate; GL is calculated on the as-reported value.

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar content with negligible fibre and potassium; standard white chocolate also carries high saturated fat load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit white chocolate consumption due to its high added sugar (9.94g/100g as reported; typically ~50g/100g in standard product) and negligible fibre and potassium, as excess added sugar independently elevates blood pressure; the HPB-recorded values appear anomalously low and the actual product should be verified against packaging before consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined sugar with zero dietary fibre and zero protein to blunt glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: White chocolate is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high refined sugar content and complete absence of dietary fibre and protein, which results in rapid glucose absorption; even at the as-reported 9.94g sugar/100g, the food provides empty calories with no glycaemic buffering, and standard white chocolate typically contains ~50g sugar/100g, far exceeding safe limits.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cocoa butter saturated fat and high added sugar; no cardioprotective polyphenols unlike dark chocolate
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: White chocolate should be avoided or strictly limited by individuals with cardiovascular disease, as it is composed primarily of cocoa butter (a saturated fat source) and refined sugar with no cardioprotective flavonoids, fibre, or omega-3 fatty acids; standard white chocolate contributes approximately 20g saturated fat/100g, which exceeds AHA daily saturated fat limits in a single moderate serving.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory saturated fat (cocoa butter) and added sugar; absence of anti-inflammatory polyphenols
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: White chocolate is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis as it contains pro-inflammatory saturated fat from cocoa butter and added sugar, while lacking the anti-inflammatory flavonoids present in dark chocolate; limit or avoid consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: White chocolate is a confectionery product made from cocoa butter, sugar, milk solids, vanilla, and lecithin. Unlike dark or milk chocolate, white chocolate contains no cocoa solids and therefore no flavonoids or theobromine. It has a smooth, creamy texture and a rich, sweet flavour with delicate vanilla notes. Standard white chocolate contains approximately 539 kcal, 30g fat (predominantly saturated from cocoa butter), and 50g sugar per 100g. The HPB Singapore Foodo record G00010629 reports anomalously low values (42 kcal, 0.25g fat, 10.36g carbohydrate per 100g), which likely reflect a diluted preparation, a white-chocolate-flavoured beverage, or a data entry discrepancy; consumers should verify against product packaging. White chocolate is ultra-processed, high in added sugar, and provides negligible fibre, protein, or micronutrients. It is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is vegetarian but not vegan due to milk solids. Common allergens include milk and soy lecithin. White chocolate is widely consumed as a snack, dessert ingredient, or confectionery gift across Western and Singaporean contexts.
common_user_queries: How many calories in white chocolate?
What is white chocolate made of?
Is white chocolate suitable for hypertension?
White chocolate vs dark chocolate nutrition

confidence_reason: The HPB Foodo record G00010629 is an approved primary source for Singapore, but the reported nutrient values (42 kcal, 0.25g fat, 10.36g carbohydrate per 100g) are anomalously low compared with standard white chocolate composition in USDA SR Legacy (~539 kcal, ~30g fat, ~59g carbohydrate per 100g). This discrepancy suggests the HPB record may represent a diluted preparation, a white-chocolate-flavoured beverage, or contain a data entry error. Values are preserved as reported but confidence is reduced to medium pending verification against product packaging or a corrected HPB entry. Saturated fat breakdown is estimated from USDA SR Legacy (NDB 19904) proportionally scaled to the reported fat value.
notes: 1. ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION (g4): protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB record G00010629; white chocolate in this preparation contains no detectable protein per the source. Cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB record. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB record — white chocolate contains no fibre by formulation. Magnesium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB record (below detection threshold, treated as trace/confirmed zero). 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (g3): vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo record G00010629 and are not available for this specific preparation in the HPB database as of 2026-04-16. 3. DATA ANOMALY: The HPB record reports 42 kcal and 0.25g fat per 100g, which is inconsistent with standard white chocolate (USDA SR Legacy NDB 19904: 539 kcal, 32.1g fat per 100g). This record likely represents a white-chocolate-flavoured beverage or diluted preparation rather than solid white chocolate. All health guidance is calibrated to standard white chocolate composition where clinically relevant. 4. SATURATED FAT ESTIMATION: saturated_fat_g (0.15g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.07g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.01g) are estimated by applying the USDA SR Legacy NDB 19904 fatty acid ratio (saturated ~60%, MUFA ~28%, PUFA ~4% of total fat) to the HPB-reported total fat of 0.25g/100g. This is documented as an adapted source match. 5. HALAL STATUS: Halal status is not confirmed as it depends on the specific product and certification; set to false pending verification. 6. GI NOTE: GI of 44 is from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for white chocolate (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). GL is calculated on the as-reported HPB carbohydrate value of 10.36g/100g for a 15g serving.

Documented all -1 sentinel fields in notes per g3 rule.
Added FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code for white chocolate with honest match types.
